identity.name: Activity Event
identity.definition: An Activity Event is a record of a single student interaction with Renaissance content — completing a skills practice session, reading a book, finishing an assignment, taking an assessment, or participating in a live lesson.

representations.description: How an Activity Event appears in activity feeds and dashboards

attributes.name: Activity Label
attributes.description: Human-readable name for the activity.

attributes.name: Activity Type
attributes.description: Category: skills practice, reading activity, assignment, assessment, or live lesson.

attributes.name: Source Product
attributes.description: Which Renaissance product generated this event.

attributes.name: Score Summary
attributes.description: Condensed score or outcome for display.

stories.title: Review Student Activity
stories.benefit: I can see what work a student has completed across all Renaissance products in a unified timeline.
stories.whenClause: I view a student's activity feed,
stories.thenClause: I see a chronological list of Activity Events with type badges, scores, and product sources.

stories.title: Drill into Activity Details
stories.benefit: I can understand exactly how a student performed and identify areas for follow-up instruction.
stories.whenClause: I open an Activity Event's detail view,
stories.thenClause: I see the full score summary, response details, content level, and a link to the source product's detailed report.

businessRules.title: Immutability
businessRules.description: Activity Events are write-once records. Once created, they cannot be edited or deleted. Score summaries may be updated as scoring completes.

businessRules.title: Student Required
businessRules.description: Every Activity Event must reference a Student ID. Anonymous or unlinked activities are not recorded.

businessRules.title: Data Freshness
businessRules.description: Events have a data freshness indicator: real_time (available immediately) or nightly_sync (available after overnight processing). Source product determines freshness.

businessRules.title: Lifecycle Progression
businessRules.description: Events progress through states: in_progress → completed → synced → scored or unscored. Not all events reach 'scored' (e.g., reading activities may remain 'completed').

states.name: In Progress
states.description: Student has started but not yet finished the activity
states.triggers: Student begins an activity in any Renaissance product

states.name: Completed
states.description: Student has finished the activity; awaiting data processing
states.triggers: Student submits or finishes the activity

states.name: Synced
states.description: Activity data has been synchronized to the central platform
states.triggers: Real-time sync or nightly batch processing completes

states.name: Scored
states.description: Activity has been scored and results are available
states.triggers: Scoring engine processes the completed activity

states.name: Unscored
states.description: Activity is complete but does not produce a score
states.triggers: Activity type does not require scoring (e.g., reading time)

relationships.mechanics: Activity Events are generated automatically when a Student interacts with Renaissance content. The Student ID is captured at event creation.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Student generates many Activity Events; each event belongs to exactly one Student.
relationships.sorts: Events for a Student: by date (most recent first, default), by activity type, by source product, by subject.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by source product, by subject, by date range, by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their Activity Events persist for historical reporting.

relationships.mechanics: Activity Events capture the Class context in which the activity was initiated. This enables class-level activity reports.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Class has many Activity Events; each event references one Class.
relationships.sorts: Events for a Class: by date, by student name, by activity type.
relationships.filters: By student, by activity type, by source product, by date range.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, Activity Events referencing it remain for historical reporting.

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: The Student who performed the activity

nestedObjects.description: The Class context for the activity

nestedObjects.name: Skill
nestedObjects.description: Skills practiced or assessed during the activity

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: The Score generated from a scored activity

allAttributes.name: Event ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Activity Event.

allAttributes.name: Student ID
allAttributes.description: The Student who performed the activity.

allAttributes.name: Class ID
allAttributes.description: The Class context in which the activity occurred.

allAttributes.name: Activity Type
allAttributes.description: Category of activity: skills_practice, reading_activity, assignment, assessment, live_lesson.

allAttributes.name: Source Product
allAttributes.description: Which Renaissance product generated this event: Freckle, myON, AR, DnA, Nearpod, Lalilo.

allAttributes.name: Activity Label
allAttributes.description: Human-readable name for the activity (e.g., 'Star Reading Assessment', 'Fraction Operations Practice').

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Subject area: Reading, Math, Science, SEL, or All.

allAttributes.name: Content Level
allAttributes.description: Difficulty or grade level of the content presented to the student.

allAttributes.name: Started At
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of when the student began the activity.

allAttributes.name: Completed At
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of when the student finished. Null if still in progress.

allAttributes.name: Data Freshness
allAttributes.description: Whether the data is real_time (immediate) or nightly_sync (batch processed).

allAttributes.name: Last Updated At
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent data update for this event.

allAttributes.name: Score Summary
allAttributes.description: Condensed score, outcome, or progress metric for display (e.g., '680L', '8/10 correct', '15 min read').

allAttributes.name: Drill Down Destination
allAttributes.description: Deep link URL to the detailed report in the source product.

allAttributes.name: Response Details
allAttributes.description: Granular item-level response data: questions answered, time per item, correctness per item.

structure.evidence: An Activity Event has rich attributes: student reference, class context, activity type, source product, timestamps, score summary, response details, and data freshness. A detail view can be designed.

instances.evidence: Harry Potter's Star Reading Assessment (680L, 35 min), Hermione's Freckle Math Practice (8/10 correct, 22 min), Ron's myON Reading Session (15 min, Charlotte's Web). Every student generates hundreds of events per year.

purpose.evidence: Activity Events power the unified activity feed in Renaissance Intelligence. Teachers use them to understand student engagement across products, track time-on-task, and identify students who need support.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object. The cross-product engagement record that unifies student activity across the Renaissance ecosystem.

synonyms.notes: Shortened form used in the activity feed interface
Technical term for the event record in the data pipeline
Used when discussing engagement metrics and usage patterns
Used in student dashboards to describe completed work

objectViews.description: Individual activity events for a student — what they worked on, when, and how they scored. Teachers scan the feed to gauge engagement and spot recent performance.
Activity events across a class listed with timestamps and scores. Teachers review what students have been doing and jump to detailed reports.
The complete activity event record — student, class, content level, timing, scores, and response details. Teachers dig into a specific activity for granular performance data.
